description_long: This is a tall, cylindrical wooden utility pole with a weathered brown surface, supporting a set of four parallel electrical wires that run horizontally across its top. The pole features a single horizontal crossarm near the top, which is equipped with insulators and hardware for securing the wires. Metal brackets and a small electrical box or fuse are attached to one side of the pole, along with a short length of cable running downward. The crossarm is reinforced with diagonal metal braces, and the insulators are spaced evenly to separate the wires. The pole tapers slightly toward the top and is embedded at the base, typical for urban and suburban power distribution. No streetlights, transformers, or identification tags are visible on the structure.
description: A tall wooden utility pole with a single horizontal crossarm, supporting four electrical wires and insulators, with metal hardware and a small attached box.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full height of the pole, four horizontal wires, and attached hardware on the right side.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the single horizontal crossarm, insulators, and the symmetrical arrangement of wires.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, with visible hardware and wires, but the attached box is less prominent.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the crossarm, insulators, and the pole's cylindrical shape with no additional attachments.
attribute_arrangement_of_crossarms: A single horizontal crossarm is mounted near the top of the pole, supporting insulators and wires in a straight, evenly spaced arrangement.
